title: OB/GYN EmergencyExam
description: OB/GYN emergency questions for EMT exam preparation

questions.text: Which of the following organs is responsible for labor and delivery of the baby?
options: cervix
uterus
placenta
perineum
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Which of the following organs provides nourishment to and removes waste products from the baby?
options: cervix
vagina
placenta
perineum
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: You have delivered an infant who is not breathing. After suctioning and physical stimulation, there are no respirations. You should:
options: administer free-flow oxygen
clamp and cut the umbilical cord
provide ventilations at a rate of 40 to 60/min
start chest compressions at a rate of 60/min
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: After birth, you notice that the infant's face and trunk appear blue. This is called:
options: pallor cyanosis
central cyanosis
peripheral cyanosis
circumferential cyanosis
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: You are rapidly transporting a patient who is at full term and presenting with a prolapsed cord. This patient is best placed:
options: on her left side
in the prone position
head up with her feet down
in Trendelenburg's position
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Premature infants are at a higher risk for:
options: breech births
infection
hyperthermia
explosive deliveries
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: While examining a patient who is in active labor, you see the umbilical cord outside the vagina, ahead of the infant's head. What should you do first?
options: Hold the mother's legs together and rapidly transport
Push the infant's head with a sterile gloved hand to relieve pressure on the cord until delivery
Gently replace as much of the cord as possible, and apply a moist dressing over the vagina
Apply gentle pressure to the head with a sterile gloved hand to prevent an explosive delivery of the cord
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: You arrive at the scene where the mother has already delivered her baby. Your partner is caring for the infant. You note a large blood loss from the mother, and she appears to still be bleeding. You should:
options: massage the uterus firmly with a circular motion
prepare the mother for transport since 1,000 mL of blood loss is normal following delivery
instruct the mother to lie with her knees drawn up and elevate her buttocks to control the bleeding
let the mother go to the bathroom and then apply vaginal pads around the vagina to soak up the remaining blood
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: You are preparing a mother and her newborn infant for transport when the placenta begins to deliver. What should you do to deliver the placenta?
options: Firmly grasp the umbilical cord and pull the placenta from the mother to help reduce her pain
Place a sterile gloved hand inside the vagina to slightly elevate the placenta and to reduce pressure on the cord
Help the mother hold her legs together to delay placenta delivery until you arrive at the hospital
Help the mother with delivery of the placenta. Bring the placenta with you to the hospital in a plastic bag
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Your patient is in active labor. You note that the infant's foot is presenting through the vagina. You should immediately:
options: elevate the patient's pelvis and rapidly transport while giving the patient oxygen
locate and massage the uterus by kneading it with your fingers fully extended
prepare the patient for delivery, as this is a reliable sign that delivery is coming soon
insert a sterile gloved hand into the vagina to relieve pressure on the umbilical cord
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Why is delivery with a prolapsed cord considered a true emergency?
options: It is a sign that the baby is too large to be delivered outside of the hospital
It is a sign of a possible miscarriage as the baby's blood supply is cut off
It might threaten the baby's life because the baby's blood supply is cut off
It might threaten the mother's life, as the decrease in blood supply might lead to seizures
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Your patient is an obviously pregnant woman complaining of abdominal pain and vaginal bleeding. Her husband says that her pain has been constant for the past 30 minutes, and there is blood all over the bed. Your exam shows that she does not appear to be crowning, and she is still bleeding from the vagina. What should you do next?
options: You should prepare for delivery because this is a predelivery emergency placenta previa
You should massage the uterus to control the bleeding, apply oxygen, and transport the patient on her left side
You should apply oxygen and prepare the patient for delivery because delivery is coming soon
You should apply oxygen and rapidly transport because there is a possible life-threatening problem with the placenta
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: As the baby's head delivers, you notice that the amniotic sac is still intact and is covering the baby's head. What should you do?
options: Prepare the mother for rapid transport, as this is a true emergency
Break open the sac, and remove the tissue from the baby's mouth and nose
Prepare for delivery of the baby's body by supporting the head with both hands
Tell the mother to push to speed up the delivery so that you can remove the tissue from the baby's mouth and nose
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: With placenta previa:
options: the mother is at a high risk for seizures
the placenta does not deliver, and the mother is at a high risk for shock
the placenta is located high in the uterus, the baby is at risk for a fast delivery, and the mother is at risk for shock
the placenta is abnormally located in the uterus. It interferes with delivery of the baby and puts the mother at risk for shock
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: How do placenta previa and abruptio placenta differ?
options: Abruptio placenta occurs in early pregnancy. Placenta previa occurs in late pregnancy
Abruptio placenta is a reliable sign that the infant is at high risk for an explosive delivery. Placenta previa signals a slow, painful delivery
Placenta previa is associated with severe abdominal pain. Abruptio placenta is associated with severe headaches
Placenta previa occurs when the placenta is covering the cervix. Abruptio placenta occurs when the placenta separates before delivery of the infant, no matter where it is located
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: When the baby's head has delivered, you should immediately:
options: apply vaginal pads to the perineal area to reduce the blood loss
massage the mother's uterus by kneading it to speed up delivery of the baby's body
insert a sterile gloved hand into the vagina to relieve pressure around the baby's neck
check the location of the umbilical cord and suction the mouth and nose
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: After delivering a baby, you should dry the baby completely. What should you do next?
options: Wrap the baby in warm blankets
Wrap the baby in sterile saline sheets
Cool the baby to stimulate crying
Cut the umbilical cord
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: After delivery, you see that the infant's face and trunk are cyanotic. The infant is crying and has a pulse of 158/min. You should:
options: cool the infant to increase its circulation
provide ventilations at a rate of 40/min
calm the infant to slow down its respirations
dry the infant and provide free-flow oxygen
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: A mother who is about to deliver tells you that she is expecting twins. You should be prepared for the possibility that:
options: both newborns will deliver quickly
they might be so small as to look premature
multiple births usually induce seizures in the mother
multiple births are associated with explosive deliveries
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Where should the umbilical cord be cut?
options: Between the two clamps
As close to the baby as possible
Just before the position of the first clamp
As close to the placenta as possible
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: You have suctioned, dried, and wrapped the newborn in a blanket. The baby is still not breathing. You should stimulate the baby by:
options: cooling it off with saline-soaked sheets
clamping and cutting the umbilical cord
flicking the soles of the feet and rubbing its back
holding it with its head slightly higher than its body
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: What is considered a breech birth?
options: The baby is born prematurely
The baby's face is the presenting part
The baby's head is too large to be delivered
The baby's buttocks are the presenting part
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Which of the following, combined with a breech birth, puts the delivery of a newborn at a higher risk?
options: prolapsed cord
face presentation
short umbilical cord
rapid labor and delivery
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: The newborn should be placed in which position until the umbilical cord is cut?
options: in the father's arms
elevated on a pillow
at the level of the vagina
on the mother's stomach
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: What care should you give a newborn that has a pulse of 90/min and respirations of 28/min?
options: Allow the mother to hold the baby to calm it
Start chest compressions at 60/min
Administer free-flow oxygen at 10 to 15 liters per minute
Ventilate the baby with a bag-valve-mask (BVM) device at 60/min
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: Pulmonary resuscitation is necessary for a newborn if:
options: breathing is absent
its breathing is rapid
the baby weighs more than 7 lb
the breathing rate is greater than 50/min
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: A mother is delivering in her home. The baby's head is crowning. You should:
options: dry the newborn's head with a sterile dressing
administer free-flow oxygen to the newborn at 10 to 15 liters per minute
apply gentle pressure to the head with your fingers to prevent an explosive delivery
apply gentle pressure to the mother's abdomen to assist with delivery of the body
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Which of the following situations would indicate adequate breathing when you assess a newborn's breathing?
options: a delivery time of less than 1 hour
a breathing rate of 20/min
a newborn with a strong cry
a newborn with peripheral cyanosis
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: As you deliver a baby, you find the umbilical cord wrapped around its neck. You should:
options: cut the cord immediately
slip the cord over the baby's head
gently pull on the baby's head to release the cord
apply pressure to the head to slow delivery and transport rapidly
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Factors that help you to assess whether a delivery is to take place soon include frequency and length of contractions, crowning, and the:
options: baby's due date
mother's complaints of nausea
mother's complaints of increased pressure in her rectum
mother's complaints of increasing pain with each contraction
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: The fetus develops inside a fluid-filled, baglike membrane called the:
options: cervix
fundus
placenta
amniotic sac
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: The part of the placenta that carries blood and nourishment to the fetus is the:
options: birth canal
peritoneum
amniotic sac
umbilical cord
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Your care for a limb presentation delivery involving an arm includes:
options: maintaining heat by gently replacing the arm in the vagina
covering the arm with a sterile towel and rapidly transporting
assisting the baby by gently pulling on the arm to adjust the delivery path
elevating the mother's buttocks and relieving pressure on the cord by inserting a sterile gloved hand
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Pregnant patients who are not in labor should be transported on the left side to avoid:
options: pressure on the uterus that could cause placenta previa
pressure on the uterus that could cause the fetus to have a bowel movement
increasing the mother's blood pressure by compressing the inferior vena cava
decreasing the mother's blood pressure by compressing the inferior vena cava
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: In a multiple birth delivery, you should:
options: plan for placenta previa
clamp and cut the umbilical cord after the first birth
plan on at least one of the fetuses being a breech delivery
begin transport if the second fetus has not delivered within 5 minutes of the first
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Clamping and cutting the umbilical cord should be completed:
options: by a doctor at the hospital
after the baby is born
by you after delivery of the placenta
as soon as the cord is visible
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: How can you assist the mother in stimulating her uterus to contract to deliver the placenta?
options: Elevate her legs 6 to 8 inches
Allow her to nurse the newborn
Give her a glass of water to drink
Apply gentle pressure to the perineum
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: When the placenta separates early from the uterine lining, it is called:
options: endometriosis
placenta previa
abruptio placenta
ectopic pregnancy
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: A delivery with a prolapsed cord means that the umbilical cord:
options: has separated early from the uterine wall
is short in length and wrapped around the unborn baby's neck
presents through the vagina before the presenting part of the baby
has caused the fetus to shift positions, leading to a limb presentation
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Care for the mother after delivery includes:
options: massaging the uterus
applying ice packs to the perineum
packing the vagina with dry, sterile dressings
applying a pneumatic antishock garment (PASG) as a precautionary measure
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: What does the term "crowning" mean?
options: The perineum has been torn
The baby is in a transverse position
The baby's head is too large for delivery
The baby's head is visible at the vagina
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: After the placenta is delivered, it should be:
options: examined for missing tissue and then thrown away
wrapped tightly with gauze and placed in a biohazard container
wrapped in a towel, kept cool over ice, and transported to the hospital for inspection
wrapped in a towel, placed in a plastic bag, and transported to the hospital for inspection
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Once the baby's head has delivered, you should:
options: clear the airway by suctioning the baby's mouth and nose
massage the mother's uterus to stimulate contractions to deliver the baby's body
gently pull on both sides of the head to assist with delivery of the body
time and assess the mother's contractions to estimate the length of the delivery
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: If a pregnant patient experiences a seizure, you should:
options: prepare for an immediate delivery
massage the uterus to relax any contractions
look for vaginal bleeding due to placenta previa
perform a rapid assessment, particularly of the airway, and transport the patient on her left side
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Care for a premature infant includes:
options: keeping the newborn warm
cooling the newborn to stimulate respirations
covering both eyes with moist, sterile dressings
withholding oxygen until arrival at the hospital
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: The hollow organ in the female reproductive system where the fetus grows is called the:
options: cervix
ovary
fallopian tube
uterus
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Which of the following statements about injuries to the female genitalia is true?
options: Foreign objects should be removed from the vagina before transport
Vaginal bleeding should be controlled by the insertion of sterile dressings
Bleeding involving the external female genitalia should be controlled with a dry, sterile dressing and direct pressure
Bleeding of the ureter can be easily controlled with direct local pressure
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: When assisting a woman during the second stage of labor, you should:
options: check for the umbilical cord around the infant's neck once the head is delivered
keep constant pressure against the infant's head from the time it presents in the vagina until it is delivered
allow the infant's head to hang dependent from the vagina without support to help the drainage of fluid from the airway
suction the nose and mouth once the infant's body is born
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Once an infant has been born, you should:
options: place it on the mother's stomach
place two clamps on the umbilical cord and cut the cord as close to the infant's abdomen as possible
keep the infant at the level of the mother's vagina
elevate the infant's head while you suction the mouth and both nostrils
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: If the amniotic sac does not rupture before an infant is born, you should:
options: immediately transport the mother to the emergency department
immediately break the membrane using a sterile clamp or your sterile, gloved fingers
break the membrane once the entire infant is born
massage the uterus to rupture the sac while protecting the infant from a gush of fluid
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: You have delivered a limp infant who is not breathing. You should first:
options: attempt endotracheal intubation
begin full CPR
attempt mouth-to-mouth-and-nose ventilations
stimulate the infant by flicking the soles of the feet
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: A woman loses approximately 500 mL of blood after delivering a healthy infant. You recognize that:
options: this is a normal amount of blood loss after delivery
the vagina must be packed with sterile gauze
the mother should be placed in the shock position
the placenta must be removed immediately by manual traction
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: En route to the hospital with a woman who is 9 months' pregnant and in active labor, you notice that the umbilical cord is prolapsed. You should first:
options: push the cord back into the vagina
place a dry, sterile towel around the cord
position the mother with her head down and/or hips raised
insert your sterile, gloved hand into the vagina and push the infant's head up
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Care of a patient who is experiencing heavy vaginal bleeding should include:
options: packing the vagina with pads
giving high-concentration oxygen
placing the patient in a semi-Fowler's position
holding the patient's legs together to slow the bleeding
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: A woman in labor tells you that she is expecting twins. The first infant is born en route to the hospital. You should:
options: clamp, but not cut, the umbilical cord of the first infant
leave the umbilical cord hanging free until the second infant is delivered
clamp and cut the umbilical cord of the first infant
apply gentle traction to the umbilical cord to assist with delivery of the second infant
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Proper care for a premature infant should include:
options: warming the patient compartment of the ambulance to 90 to 95F
placing hot water bottles on the infant to keep it warm
giving oxygen with an infant-sized face mask
allowing the mother, father, and any other family members to handle the infant
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: Why is it important to keep a newborn warm while providing treatment?
options: A newborn has difficulty regulating body temperature
It is easier for you to provide treatment in a warm environment
Oxygen will flow from an oxygen source with little difficulty in a warm environment
The warm environment will make the newborn sleepy, making transport easier
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: When you compare a primagravida vs. a multigravida woman, a general rule of thumb is that a primagravida's length of the first stage of labor is:
options: longer than a multigravida
shorter than a multigravida
equal to a multigravida
difficult to actually compare
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Para refers to the number of:
options: pregnancies
live births
total births
miscarriages
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: A 35-year-old woman, 32 weeks' gestation, reports increased swelling in her hands and feet the last week and now complains of a sudden, intense headache. Her vital signs are as follows: pulse of 92/min; respirations of 24/min; and blood pressure of 144/92 mm Hg. You are concerned she has:
options: hypertensive syndrome
pre-eclampsia
eclampsia
vitamin B12 deficiency
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: A 26-year-old woman complains of vaginal bleeding, heavier than usual and a sharp pain in her RLQ. She reports her last menstrual period was 7 weeks ago and has a history of pelvic inflammatory disease. You are suspicious she has:
options: an exacerbation of her pelvic inflammatory disease
an acute placenta abruptio
a ruptured ectopic pregnancy
pregnancy-induced hypertension
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Which of the following would NOT be an indication of imminent birth?
options: The mother feels the urge to move her bowels
Crowning
Gushing of amniotic fluid
The mother states she needs to push
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Which of the following is an indication of imminent birth?
options: Amniotic sac ruptures
The mother states the baby is coming
Contractions are 10 minutes apart and lasting 30 seconds
The mother instinctively begins to clean the house and pack for the hospital
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: While preparing a pregnant woman to deliver in the ambulance, she should be positioned:
options: flat on her back with her legs bent
in a semi-Fowler's position with padding under her hips
squatting with 2-3 pillows underneath her
on her left side with the right leg elevated
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Greenish amniotic fluid is an indication of:
options: fetal demise
fetal distress
placental insufficiency
fetal prematurity
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: Thick meconium staining is most likely to result in:
options: a neonatal infection
a maternal infection
aspiration and respiratory distress
heavy vaginal bleeding after delivery
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: Calculate an APGAR score for the following infant. Body is pink with acrocyanosis. Pulse is 88/min and respirations are absent. Infant does not cry or move when stimulated, and muscle tone is weak.
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: You have just delivered a baby boy. His appearance is pink and his pulse is 148/min with slow respirations. He has a weak cry when stimulated and resists attempts to straighten his legs. His APGAR score would be?
questions.subject: Pediatrics

questions.text: Prolapsed cords are more common with which presentation?
options: breech
limb
posterior
vertex
questions.subject: OB/GYN

questions.text: A 17-year-old woman has delivered a small infant breech. The head remains in the birth canal. The EMT-B should immediately:
options: contact medical control for directions
press firmly on the lower abdomen to push the head out
apply gentile traction to the infant's body
make a V with your fingers and create an airway for the infant
questions.subject: OB/GYN
